components.name: H3BO3 (boric acid)
components.evidence: trace elements (134 μM EDTA, 31 μM FeCl3·6H2O, 6.2 μM ZnCl2, 0.76 μM CuCl2·2H2O, 0.42 μM CoCl2·2H2O, 1.62 μM H3BO3, 0.081 μM MnCl2·4H2O)

components.name: MnCl2·4H2O (manganese chloride)
components.evidence: trace elements (134 μM EDTA, 31 μM FeCl3·6H2O, 6.2 μM ZnCl2, 0.76 μM CuCl2·2H2O, 0.42 μM CoCl2·2H2O, 1.62 μM H3BO3, 0.081 μM MnCl2·4H2O)

components.name: D-Gluconate (sodium gluconate)
components.evidence: Carbon sources were added as described in the text at a concentration of 20 mM. [Specific carbon sources tested included ribose, xylose, and gluconate, all at 20 mM]

components.name: O2 (dioxygen, air)
components.evidence: cultures were grown in an orbital shaker set to maintain 37 °C and an atmosphere of 20% CO2 mixed with air

added: complete 7-component trace element mix (EDTA, FeCl3, ZnCl2, CuCl2, CoCl2, H3BO3, MnCl2)

media.notes: Recipe is fully self-contained in the paper's Methods; no external base-medium reference is cited, so nothing needed chasing. The trace-element solution has exactly 7 components (EDTA + Fe, Zn, Cu, Co, B, Mn) with no molybdate/nickel/selenite/tungstate and no vitamins. EDTA and H3BO3 were previously uncovered (no exchange mapping) but are genuine medium constituents. Cultivation is aerobic at 37 °C (shaking); some experiments used 20% CO2 in air.

EDTA and H3BO3 were previously uncovered but are genuine constituents. Aerobic, 37 °C.
